programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: A Friday night shift proves a hectic time for the Sun Hill coppers as a childhood friend of Amber's is raped, a drug-dealer is collared and a mugging victim is attacked for a second time - and in the aftermath of all the excitement, Phil's unruly hormones are fired by an encounter with a fellow sex addict. A guilt-ridden Honey decides to tell Steve their relationship is over, Neil pours out his heart to Andrea, and Yvonne learns she's no longer Acting Sergeant - leading to much speculation about her replacement(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Jaws drop at Sun Hill as June Ackland returns to take up the post of sergeant, a mere two months after her retirement. It's no secret she needs the money to clear Jim's gambling debts - but she makes it plain she's not prepared to be a figure of fun or pity. Meanwhile, Gary finds himself implicated in a money-laundering operation after falling for a woman's charms, and Andrea agonises over whether to tell Neil the reason for her refusal to commit to a relationship(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: June leaps to Roger's defence after the leader of a gang of rowdy schoolgirls accuses him of indecent assault, and later plants a kiss on the startled copper - leaving an ominous question mark hanging over her already troubled relationship with Jim. Gary swears revenge on the female con-artist who inveigled him into the money laundering scam, and Leela is dismayed to learn Amber will be joining her on an advanced driving course, while Andrea tries to avoid exposure as an undercover journalist by promising her editor a scoop on Gabriel's murderous misdeeds(n)

Detective drama, starring Patricia Routledge, Richard Pasco and Suzanne Maddock(n)

programme.desc: A water shortage means that the maternity home is busier than ever and two old schoolmates are reunited, but Marion shuns Shirley's attempts at renewing their friendship until they are brought together later in an unexpected way. programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Trixie and Sister Mary Cynthia team up to support a deaf mother-to-be, while another patient causes great concern when a case of morning sickness proves more serious than initially suspected. On the personal front, handyman Fred's daughter is less than delighted to hear about his forthcoming wedding. Will he be forced to choose between her and Violet?(n)

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Gerry is forced to confront his past when the team reinvestigates the 33-year-old murder of a doctor whose body was found hanging from a meat hook on a butcher's stall at Smithfield Market. The butcher had gone missing on the same day, and since then the community has rallied to help his widow keep the business afloat. However, his recently discovered remains reveal a match to an unidentified person and lead to a shocking revelation for Sandra. Guest starring Ken Farrington, David Calder and Diana Quick, with Amanda Redman and Alun Armstrong(n)

Final episode of the crime drama, starring Kenneth Branagh(n)

Meanwhile, Brian needs to find a handyman when Esther bans him from his latest DIY project. Hugh Dennis and Samuel West guest star, with Amanda Redman(n)

programme.title: Spy in the Wild
programme.desc: In the final episode of the series, the spy cameras travel to the Arctic and Antarctic circles, following chicks in a king penguin colony on the island of South Georgia in the Southern Ocean. On the other side of the world, Spy Walrus discovers a walrus family hauled out on an ice floe, before Spy Gyrfalcon follows the family as they head towards dry land. Elsewhere on Norway's Hornoya island, Spy Puffin follows thousands of male puffins as they gather in the snow and search for their life-long partners(n)

programme.title: Countryfile

programme.title: Doctor Who

But in the face of such a relentless enemy, has she put her best friends at risk? What terrors lie hiding in the depths of space, and what is Ko Sharmus? Guest starring Julie Graham, Patrick O'Kane and Steve Toussaint(n)

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Sister Julienne decides it is time to take matters in hand when a letter arrives from the council saying that it will cut funding to Nonnatus House and no longer pay for the accommodation, and goes to see Violet at one of her open sessions as a local councillor. At clinic, Nurse Crane meets and cares for heavily pregnant Bonnie, who is not married but committed to the baby's father Eddie, while Kevin's dependency on pain relief is discovered by the team and Dr Turner and Miss Higgins must find a way to help him(n)

programme.title: Last Tango in Halifax
programme.desc: Alan and Celia are not seeing eye to eye seven years into their marriage, while tempers fray at Caroline and Gillian's joint birthday when Celia can't hold her tongue. Return of Sally Wainwright's drama, starring Derek Jacobi, Anne Reid, Sarah Lancashire, Nicola Walker, Tony Gardner and Dean Andrews(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day 2

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Graham brings the 26th series of his hit chat show to an end by dipping back into its archives to recall some of the more memorable verbal exchanges. Stars appearing include Robert De Niro, Bruce Springsteen, Arnold Schwarzenegger, Nadiya Hussain, Emilia Clarke, Regina King, Julie Andrews, Jennifer Aniston, Reese Witherspoon, Olivia Colman, Helena Bonham Carter, Elizabeth Banks, Ricky Gervais, Lewis Hamilton, Hillary Clinton, Dwayne Johnson, Kevin Hart, Jodie Whittaker, Judi Dench, Jennifer Hudson, Hugh Grant, Daisy Ridley, Ruth Jones, Motsi Mabuse, Anthony Joshua and Tom Hanks(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather. Presented by Dan Walker and Louise Minchin(n)

programme.title: Britain's Housing Scandal
programme.desc: Matt Allwright meets a Merseyside woman who has lived without hot water for 10 years, and East Sussex council teams investigate a property being illegally used to house workers(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell, Dion Dublin and Martin Roberts visit a terrace house in Middlesex, a flat in Staffordshire, and a plot of land in Bargoed, Wales, that are being sold at auction(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under Revisited
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman catches up with the Mudhar family, who spent a trial week in Melbourne, to see if they decided to make the move or stay put in London(n)

programme.title: Defenders UK
programme.desc: The enforcement officers investigate the death of a seven-year-old girl when the bouncy castle she was playing on was swept away by a gust of wind(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Raj Bisram and Stephanie Connell help two teams scour Malvern antiques and collectors fair to pick up items to sell at auction, while host Eric Knowles meets a photographer attempting to capture images of the UK's remaining art deco cinemas(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Karen urges Abz to tell the truth about their relationship, Jimmi is given hope, and a child psychologist threatens to bring her work home with her(n)

programme.title: Shakespeare & Hathaway - Private Investigators
programme.desc: Frank and Lu investigate a missing person's case at a casino, where gambling is a matter of life or death. Starring Mark Benton and Jo Joyner(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Margherita Taylor helps a retired couple from Essex find a new home in Norfolk on a budget of £350,000, and also discovers the county's answer to the Great Barrier Reef(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Northern Ireland's only buffalo farmers Barry and Michael, and rapeseed oil producers Jane and Michael, hope to do well at the 40th annual Irish Game Fair(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: It is day two, and Catherine Southon and Paul Laidlaw are motoring their open-topped roadster along the byways of Worcestershire, Herefordshire, Powys and Gloucestershire(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat. Presented by Alex Jones and Matt Baker(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Gray feels the pressure after agreeing to take on Whitney's case and snaps at Mackenzie and Mia, leaving Chantelle worried. Following a conversation with Daniel, Jean hatches a plan to cheer him up. However, her mood changes following a phone call as she enlists help from Mo and Kush(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Ric is determined to do anything he can to help the relative of a former patient, despite the AAU being filled to capacity and the hospital being in the midst of a nursing crisis. Essie realises that she has to decide where she stands with Sasha when Ben makes a grand gesture. Kian returns from Holby from Canada to discover that Jac has been taking a step back from her work, and resolves to force her out of her comfort zone(n)

programme.title: The Split
programme.desc: Hannah finds her personal and professional lives colliding when she is forced to work with Nathan and his new protegee on the Hansen case. She desperately tries to clean up any evidence of her affair, only for Christie to uncover a secret about her past that leaves him consumed with anger - and secrets threaten to spill out at an anniversary party. The lawyers attend their first meeting with Richie's representatives to discuss childcare arrangements, which leaves Hannah concerned that her opponents will fight dirty and that Fi has not told her everything. Guest starring Anna Chancellor(n)

programme.title: Plastic Surgery Undressed
programme.desc: Four people contemplating cosmetic surgery get to witness a tummy tuck procedure being performed, and question if they still want to go through with it themselves. The audience includes a man who has halved his bodyweight but been left with excess skin, a woman struggling to regain her figure after giving birth to twins(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Crystal Palace left-back Patrick van Aanholt, former Premier League star Jimmy Floyd Hasselbaink, England cricketer Samit Patel and tennis player Naomi Broady join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: The Instant Gardener
programme.desc: Presenter Helen Skelton and expert garden designer Danny Clarke transform a garden in Solihull to provide a special boy with a wealth of sensory stimulation(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Comedian Bobby Davro and songstress Sheila Ferguson of the Three Degrees compete to find profit-making antiques in Kent, guided by experts James Braxton and Catherine Southon. Away from the contest, Bobby hears the little-known history of the Royal Engineers, while Sheila discovers how black culture and music arrived - and thrived - in the Garden of England(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher meets Essex farmer David Eagle, who has let the sea inundate acres and acres of his farmland to create vital saltmarsh habitat for migratory birds(n)

programme.title: Beechgrove Repotted
programme.desc: Visiting gardens and people who see the garden as an artist's palette, from a topiary zoo to a woman who makes her art out of other people's rubbish. Plus, a visit to EA Hornell's garden in Kirkcudbright(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents highlights of the series, recalling his favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen(n)

programme.title: Rick Stein: From Venice to Istanbul
programme.desc: The chef continues his journey through Croatia and savours the native oysters of Ston, before crossing the border into Albania, where he is joined by his son Jack and the pair sample local dishes such as chicken jufka and kokoretsi. In his own kitchen on the Greek island of Symi, Rick cooks traditional Albanian dishes, including Lepur Comlek and Tave Kosi(n)

programme.title: Best Bakes Ever
programme.desc: In light of Britain's baking obsession, actress and cake expert Jane Asher presents a selection of recipes from television chefs, including James Martin, Mary Berry, Paul Hollywood, the Hairy Bikers, Raymond Blanc, Nigella Lawson, Tom Kerridge, Lorraine Pascale and Rachel Khoo(n)

programme.title: Trust Me, I'm a Doctor
programme.desc: In the final episode, Michael Mosley investigates whether simply thinking about food can help you lose weight, and Giles Yeo finds out how to keep your skin young and healthy. Meanwhile, Alain Gregoire tests whether listening to music improves your sleep, and Guddi Singh examines the latest diet in the headlines which claims to have a good impact on the planet(n)

programme.title: Painting the Holy Land
programme.desc: Lachlan Goudie explores the role of Mary in the Bible, and why she has become one of the figures in Christianity most heavily represented in art(n)

programme.title: Dunkirk (1958)
programme.desc: Soldiers are separated from their comrades-in-arms during the Dunkirk evacuation and face a terrifying journey to safety. A heroic lance corporal takes charge, aiming to lead the platoon to sanctuary while those around him panic. Second World War adventure, starring John Mills, Richard Attenborough, Robert Urquhart and Bernard Lee(n)

programme.title: Undiscovered Worlds with Steve Backshall
programme.desc: Steve Backshall searches for remains of the Mayan civilisation in the Yucatan, exploring a network of underground caves that were believed to be a supernatural world. The expedition uncovers a flooded cavern filled with the remains of human sacrifice victims, as well as artefacts that reveal a less disturbing side to this ancient culture(n)

programme.title: Six Nations Highlights
programme.desc: John Inverdale presents action from the third round of fixtures, featuring Italy v Scotland at Stadio Olimpico, Wales v France at Principality Stadium and England v Ireland at Twickenham. Italy and Scotland were aiming for their first wins of the campaign, while France and Ireland faced tough tests as they looked to maintain their Grand Slam hopes(n)

programme.title: Russia with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: On the 100th anniversary of the Russian Revolution, adventurer Simon Reeve embarks on a journey across the world's biggest country. He begins in Russia's far east, and the snow-capped volcanoes of Kamchatka and Yakutia, where reindeer herders live in temperatures as low as minus 30. Outside the eastern port of Vladivostok, Simon investigates the growing influence of China on the area, then passes through the Boreal Forest, where he meets a conservationist dedicated to protecting the Amur tiger. His final destination is a giant hole in the ground in Siberia(n)

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness, Freddie Flintoff and Chris Harris take the latest sports cars from Ferrari, Aston Martin and Porsche on a Yorkshire road trip. Back on the track, Chris tests out Volkswagen's ID-R electric racer. YouTube star KSI is the studio guest(n)

programme.title: Win the Wilderness: Alaska
programme.desc: Duane and Rena question each couple about their plans for the home to see if any of them are harbouring doubts about their ability to make a success of life in the wilderness. This round of questioning results in one couple being eliminated from the contest, while the remaining three are separated from each other for two days to test their ability to cope with isolation(n)

programme.title: The Ranganation
programme.desc: Romesh Ranganathan hosts a topical comedy show, in which a diverse group of 25 members of the public drawn from all walks of life are invited to share their thoughts on the state of modern Britain today. Romesh and his outspoken and passionate focus group will be joined in the studio by Jon Richardson(n)

programme.title: Frankie Boyle's Tour of Scotland
programme.desc: The comedian explores Glasgow, calling in at a martial arts club in the east end and exploring the ruins of Crookston Castle. Frankie's home city is no stranger to riots and protest and he meets one high-profile campaigner who became famous as one of the 'Glasgow Girls'. On the journey to his stand-up show at the Gaiety Theatre in Ayr, Frankie pops into the town of Troon to meet some members of one of the most famous showmen families in Scotland. Upon reaching Ayr, he catches up with fellow performer Elaine C Smith to discuss performing onstage and their different styles of comedy(n)

programme.title: Noah (2014)
programme.desc: A man is chosen by God to build an ark capable of housing his family and pairs of every living creature when the world is flooded. However, Noah's mission is complicated by his adopted daughter Ila, and the villainous Tubal-cain, who plans on storming the vessel to escape the Creator's vengeance. Biblical epic, starring Russell Crowe, Ray Winstone and Emma Watson(n)

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate from Weymouth, inviting the studio audience to question a panel of politicians and other guests on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week(n)

programme.title: Fraud Squad NHS
programme.desc: Michelle Ackerley presents a programme following the work of investigators in the NHS Counter Fraud Authority, beginning with the case of a hospital worker who stole more than £1,000,000 worth of surgical equipment to sell online(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: A reconciliation between Chloe and Cameron seems unlikely after the previous night's events, but nevertheless, he decides to make a bold move to win back her trust. A familiar face returns to Holby to perform surgery on Carole, and Dominic finds a much-needed source of moral support as he waits for the outcome. Jac makes plans for her first unsupervised visit from Emma, but things soon get out of control(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under Revisited
programme.desc: Jayne Myburgh thought her son Albie's rare genetic disorder would be greater controlled in the warmth of the Australian sun. Her South African-born husband Eugene was well aware of his wife's dreams, but needed more than a promise of sunshine to convince him he should move to the other side of the world. Nicki Chapman finds out whether or not they made the life-changing decision to relocate Down Under(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross hosts from the Agricultural Showground on Anglesey, where antique experts Roo Irvine and Richard Madley guide two teams as they search for items of interest and value to take to auction. Away from the contest, Charlie also meets a familiar face with a passion for Georgian glass(n)

programme.title: Best Home Cook
programme.desc: Mary Berry asks the contestants to produce their ultimate meringue dessert for a family celebration lunch - leading to stunning pavlovas, inventive fruit curd pies and even a woodland-themed roulade. This is followed by Chris Bavin's one-hour challenge to rustle up a dish from scratch featuring a humble vegetable as the star of the show, before the judges decide who has to take on Angela Hartnett's elimination cook for their place in the contest - a single recipe that requires precision timing and perfect seasoning. Claudia Winkleman hosts(n)

programme.title: Victoria Derbyshire
programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Jo Coburn and guests discuss the big political issues of the day(n)

programme.title: Women's Six Nations Highlights
programme.desc: Action from the third round of fixtures, featuring Wales v France at Cardiff Arms Park, England v Ireland at Castle Park and Italy v Scotland at Stadio Giovanni Mari(n)

programme.title: Chase the Case
programme.desc: Strategic game show hosted by Dan Walker in which five contestants trade general knowledge for information to gain access to a case containing a secret amount of cash(n)

programme.title: Best House in Town
programme.desc: Homeowners in York open their doors to five neighbours, featuring a Victorian cottage, a 1960s farmhouse given a new lease of life and a 1980s bungalow(n)

programme.title: Red Rock
programme.desc: Sharon and Rory are torn apart by the consequences of her claim, while the Kielys try to find a solution. Patricia is pestered about her business status(n)

programme.title: The Great British Winter
programme.desc: Ecologist Ellie Harrison explores Britain's most extreme winter landscapes, beginning with mountain terrain. Along the way, she reveals surprises that lie in store throughout the territory as she encounters seemingly out-of-place volcanos, avalanches and small herds of reindeer(n)

programme.title: Big Blue UK
programme.desc: Hugh Fearnley-Whittingstall and a team of experts detail the lives of marine animals visiting and thriving in UK waters each summer, beginning by finding out where on the coast it is possible to see dolphins and whales. With contributions by wildlife cameraman Richard Taylor-Jones and BBC Springwatch Extra presenter Lindsey Chapman(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: In Blackburn, Anita Manning values a gold charm bracelet and Philip Serrell takes a shine to an unusual globe. Paul Martin finds about the life of classical singer Kathleen Ferrier(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Samira Ahmed, Dom Joly, Angela Rippon and John Robins go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Armed with his early 20th century Bradshaw's Guide, Michael Portillo sets off on the first leg of a trip through Britain's industrial heartlands in the footsteps of George V, beginning by travelling from Warrington to Preston(n)

programme.title: Coastal Path
programme.desc: Paul Rose sets off on the walk of a lifetime - 630 miles of the South West Coast Path. The explorer hopes to discover wildlife, wild traditions and wild adventure at every turn(n)

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys invites four more contenders to brave the black chair, answering questions on the specialist subjects of the Undertones, Dad's Army, Genghis Khan and Basil Rathbone's Sherlock Holmes films. They then each have two minutes to demonstrate their general knowledge(n)

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell asks the questions as the 007s take on the Junipers in the third of the quarter-final matches, with a place in the semi-finals at stake. They must use patience, lateral thinking and sheer inspiration to make connections between groups of four things that may appear at first not to be linked, with one set of clues consisting of Harris, Dean Martin, Inspector Morse, and Frank Duckworth(n)

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: In the fifth of the quarter-finals, two more teams of four students compete to make it to the next stage of the competition as they try to win two matches and progress to the semi-finals. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions(n)

programme.title: Murder 24/7
programme.desc: Documentary following murder investigations by Essex police, from the critical first few days right through to arrest and conviction. It begins with the first of three programmes charting the inquiry into the fatal stabbing of 36-year-old Courtney Valentine-Brown in Southend-on-Sea, Essex, as a team of detectives and specialists work around the clock to catch the killers(n)

programme.title: Inside No 9
programme.desc: Student journalist Gabriel is determined to interview world-famous magician Neville Griffin, who is renowned for being extremely protective of his secrets and believes stealing a magic trick is a crime worse than murder. But how would he know that, exactly? The latest instalment of the darkly comic drama anthology, written by and starring The League of Gentlemen's Steve Pemberton and Reece Shearsmith, with guest appearances by Fionn Whitehead, Jill Halfpenny and Tom Goodman-Hill(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis(n)

programme.title: Hospital
programme.desc: At Aintree Hospital's major trauma centre, a 15-strong team try to save the life of a young man who has arrived by emergency helicopter. He has been stabbed in the chest - one of three stabbings to arrive during the day. Across the city at the Royal Liverpool University Hospital, staff deal with a homeless man who has been returning for at least 18 years. He is always treated with patience and dignity and given somewhere to sleep his alcohol or substance misuse. However, his increasingly aggressive behaviour leads to him being issued with an ultimatum(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Two inner-city London charities head to the 46th Lambeth Country Show. This is their chance to raise awareness, funds and support so they can continue their vital community work(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher meets Essex farmer David Eagle, who has let the sea inundate acres and acres of his farmland to create vital saltmarsh habitat for migratory birds(n)

programme.title: Secrets of the Museum
programme.desc: Project manager Alice and her team unframe and scan a gallery of 500-year-old masterpieces - Raphael's cartoons, removing frames that have sealed them in for 30 years. While in the V&A's stunning jewellery galleries, newly-appointed keeper James must check some of the most fragile objects in the collection - the jewellery made with moving parts. Among the treasures are a spectacular butterfly ring worn by Beyoncé that appears to take flight with each gentle flex of the finger(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under Revisited
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman catches up with an Australian-born man and his Colombian-born, wife whose trial week in Brisbane a year previously had proved traumatic for their daughter(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Charles Hanson and Philip Serrell hunt for objects to take to auction at Blackburn Cathedral, Lancashire. Charles values a wooden Fijian club, while two vintage enamel signs catch Philip's eye. Presenter Paul Martin takes a look at the impressive Talbot collection - local press photographs taken of events, celebrities and the royal family over a 50-year period(n)

programme.title: Spy in the Wild
programme.desc: Camouflaged spy cameras go 'undercover' in the animal world. For the first time, cameras follow a family of gorillas, with Spy Grub and Spy Fruit testing their intelligence. Elsewhere in Brazil, Spy Jaguar Cub explores a beautiful gathering of water birds, capybara and caiman, and joins Spy Caiman to film a feeding frenzy(n)

programme.title: Victoria Derbyshire
programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Jo Coburn and guests discuss the big political issues of the day(n)

programme.title: Lifeline
programme.desc: An appeal on behalf of the charity StreetGames, which harnesses the power of sport to create positive change in the lives of disadvantaged young people across the UK.(n)

programme.title: The Super League Show
programme.desc: Tanya Arnold introduces action from the World Club Challenge between St Helens and Sydney Roosters at Totally Wicked Stadium. Plus, a round-up of the latest Super League fixtures, including Castleford Tigers v Wakefield Trinity, Wigan Warriors v Hull FC and Warrington Wolves v Toronto Wolfpack(n)

programme.title: Best House in Town
programme.desc: Four York apartments, including a modern penthouse with a killer view, go head-to-head in order to impress five local judges and be chosen to compete in this week's final(n)

programme.title: Beechgrove Repotted
programme.desc: A look at the transformation of Edinburgh's Saughton Park from a neglected site into a model educational facility with a physic garden(n)

programme.title: Red Rock
programme.desc: Patricia encounters a charming politician, Keith has a deal complicated by a face from his past, and it remains to be seen how far Jim will go to protect Rory(n)

programme.title: The Great British Winter
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison visits the Lake District to explore how its landscape and wildlife are affected by Britain's changeable weather. During her journey, she encounters a rare Arctic winter resident and witnesses a lakeside spectacle performed by a family of otters(n)

programme.title: Big Blue UK
programme.desc: Hugh Fearnley-Whittingstall and his team find out where sharks can be spotted in waters surrounding the UK, while Lindsay Chapman looks for shark egg cases washed up on our shore. Can she also find live shark embryos in amongst Devon's seaweed?(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin and the team are at Glemham Hall in the Suffolk countryside, where David Fletcher spots a rare flintlock pistol, and James Lewis uncovers a copy of a Raphael painting(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Samira Ahmed, Dom Joly, Angela Rippon and John Robins go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: In Blackburn, Michael Portillo catches a rare glimpse of Edwardian life on celluloid and marvels at how factory workers and schoolchildren alike were drawn to seek fame on film. Continuing east to Nelson, Michael braves the enemy camp to have a pint of tea with the socialist working classes in Britain's last Clarion House(n)

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness, Freddie Flintoff and Chris Harris take the latest sports cars from Ferrari, Aston and Porsche on a Yorkshire road trip. Back on the track, Chris tests out Volkswagen's ID-R electric racer. YouTube star KSI is the studio guest(n)

programme.title: Back in Time for...
programme.desc: A family of five works in a corner shop to reveal how such businesses have changed over the course of the past 100 years, aided by presenter Sara Cox and social historian Polly Russell. The first episode covers shops from the Victorian era to the First World War. The family must learn to bake their own counter goods, measure loose products like tea, flour and sugar by hand, and make deliveries with a horse and cart. They also receive a visit from Kelly Holmes, who puts them through an Edwardian workout and discover the impact of food shortages on corner shops as the war drew on(n)

programme.title: Murder 24/7
programme.desc: A man is arrested for the murder of Courtney Valentine-Brown, but during his interview, he names another as being the killer. This sparks a manhunt to locate him, but the search is against the clock, as detectives must find the second suspect before it becomes necessary to either charge or release the first(n)

programme.title: Better Things
programme.desc: Phil is disappointed when Sam says she is not accompanying her to Walter's Easter egg party, but she takes Duke instead and tells her some inappropriate family information(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: The day's national and international news stories, presented by Emily Maitlis(n)

programme.title: Forensics: The Real CSI
programme.desc: A man is violently assaulted and stabbed in his home by two unknown assailants in Newcastle, and rookie Nicola has to examine the crime scene for clues to identify the attackers. Meanwhile in Sunderland, a woman reports being raped in a block of flats, and Intimate samples are immediately taken to provide the best chance of identifying the unknown suspects and bringing them to justice. With two different, and as yet unknown people's DNA present in the samples, identifying individual DNA profiles for comparison with the national DNA database is no simple matter(n)

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Reshmin Chowdhury presents highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League, including Arsenal v Reading at Meadow Park and Manchester City v Chelsea at Academy Stadium, as the title race between the Gunners, City and the Blues continued(n)

programme.title: Iolo's Snowdonia
programme.desc: As winter arrives, Iolo endures life-threatening wind chill, ice and snow on the Carneddau high peaks. A cold snap takes him to one of his old roaming grounds on the Berwyn moors, where he finds a solitary robin surviving in thick snow. Opposite Portmeirion, the Dwyryd estuary is teeming with birds feeding. Iolo also heads to Ceunant Llennyrch, a gorge hidden by an ancient woodland that has existed since trees first colonised Snowdonia after the last ice age(n)

programme.title: Attenborough: 60 Years in the Wild
programme.desc: David Attenborough reflects on environmental changes during his film-making career, the pioneering conservationists whose footsteps he followed and the revolution in attitudes toward nature. On a tropical beach in Malaysia, David recalls how in the 1950s he dug up turtle eggs to eat and releases newly hatched turtles into the wild. Finally he asks whether changing attitudes and greater knowledge of the world will be enough to save it for future generations(n)

programme.title: Art on the BBC
programme.desc: Art historian Rose Balston explores six decades of BBC archive to discover how TV has influenced our understanding of John Constable. The artist pushed the boundaries of landscape painting, to the extent he was rejected by the establishment, but, perhaps just as surprisingly, he poured his love for one woman on to the canvas as no painter of landscapes had done before(n)

programme.title: Britain's Lost Masterpieces
programme.desc: Art experts Jacky Klein and Dr Bendor Grosvenor visit Haddo House in Aberdeenshire, which may be the home to some of Scotland's greatest lost paintings. They also head to the storerooms of Montrose Museum, where a portrait of Richard Mead purported to be a copy may actually be the original(n)

programme.title: Britain's Outlaws: Highwaymen, Pirates and Rogues
programme.desc: Historian Sam Willis explores the criminal underworld of Britain in the 17th and 18th centuries, beginning with the arrival of the highwayman in the wake of the English Civil War. Heavily romanticised in literature, these glamorous gangsters became a social menace on the roads and a political thorn in the side of the creaking British state, but a far darker reality lay underneath the dashing image of stylish robbers on horseback(n)

programme.title: Mackintosh: Glasgow's Neglected Genius
programme.desc: Glasgow artist Lachlan Goudie examines the life, work and legacy of Scotland's most celebrated architect and designer, Charles Rennie Mackintosh. The programme examines Mackintosh's iconic buildings, most notably the Glasgow School of Art(n)

programme.title: Saints and Sinners: Britain's Millennium of Monasteries
programme.desc: Dr Janina Ramirez discovers how monasteries shaped every aspect of medieval Britain and created a dazzling array of art, architecture and literature. She begins her journey on the desolate rock of Skellig Michael, off Ireland's east coast, which is home to the oldest surviving monastery in the British Isles. She investigates the lives led by the early monks and charts the arrival of hermetic Irish monasticism in Anglo-Saxon Northumberland, revealing why monasteries in places such as Lindisfarne and Whitby became beacons of civilisation and literature(n)

programme.title: Raiders of the Lost Past with Janina Ramirez
programme.desc: Dr Janina Ramirez heads to Mexico, where, just before the outbreak of the Second World War, American husband-and-wife explorer team Matthew and Marion Stirling were lured into the jungle by the legend of a colossal stone head. They found the head - and a lot more than they bargained for - because it turned out to be the first clue in a trail that led to the discovery of a lost civilisation, now known as the Olmecs(n)

programme.title: Art on the BBC
programme.desc: Art historian Rose Balston explores six decades of BBC archive to discover how TV has influenced our understanding of John Constable. The artist pushed the boundaries of landscape painting, to the extent he was rejected by the establishment, but, perhaps just as surprisingly, he poured his love for one woman on to the canvas as no painter of landscapes had done before(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: Francesco's Mediterranean Voyage
programme.desc: Francesco da Mosto faces a difficult journey as he manoeuvres under the Rio Antirio bridge and navigates the narrow Corinth Canal. He stops off at Mount Parnassus to visit the mystical site of the Oracle at Delphi, before observing sacred temple the Parthenon on the Acropolis overlooking Athens and meeting singer Nana Mouskouri in the National Gardens of Greece(n)

programme.title: New Zealand: Earth's Mythical Islands
programme.desc: Sam Neill narrates the story of some of New Zealand's wildest places, home to hyper-intelligent parrots, snails with teeth, and constellations of glow-worms. In these often inhospitable locations, any creature hoping to survive must resort to radical techniques, none more so than on the South Island, which lies toward Antarctica and is home to the rapidly rising peaks of the Southern Alps(n)

programme.title: Hunting the Nazi Gold Train
programme.desc: In August 2015 two amateur researchers declared they had found a lost Nazi train buried in a railway cutting near Walbrzych in Silesia, southern Poland. This followed decades of local folklore that a train, loaded with gold, had vanished after leaving Wroclaw in early 1945. Dan Snow follows the treasure hunters as they embark on the hunt, while also investigating this extraordinary story that leads all the way to Adolf Hitler himself and his secret plans for this remote corner of the Third Reich during the closing months of the Second World War(n)

programme.title: Storyville
programme.desc: Documentary filmed over the course of seven years, charting Jonathan Agassi's journey from prolific adult movie star to male escort while battling many demons, exploring the deeper and more devastating reasons for Agassi's self-destructive behaviour(n)

programme.title: Art of France
programme.desc: Andrew Graham-Dixon examines the history of French art, revealing how it emerged from a struggle between tradition and revolution, and rulers and citizens. Starting with the invention of Gothic architecture, he traces its development up until the arrival of Classicism and the Age of Enlightenment(n)

programme.title: Saints and Sinners: Britain's Millennium of Monasteries
programme.desc: Dr Janina Ramirez continues to chart how monasteries shaped every aspect of medieval Britain as she looks at the golden age of the British monastery - the medieval period. Monks transformed British society, and subsequently rose to a position of immense power. Over 500 British monastic houses were established by the 14th century, and monks became pioneers in the fields of medicine, science, scholarship, industry, farming, art and music. However, their inevitable wealth, combined with the sins of individual monks left the religious institutions they called home on the brink of a catastrophic and total collapse(n)

programme.title: Rude Britannia
programme.desc: Documentary disproving the national stereotype of Britain as a polite country of restraint and decorum, exploring traditions of satire, lewd humour and a taste for ridicule. This episode examines the early 18th century and travels through the Georgian years to the accession of Queen Victoria in 1837, considering the graphic art of Hogarth and Gillray, and the poetry of Alexander Pope and Lord Byron(n)

programme.title: New Zealand: Earth's Mythical Islands
programme.desc: Sam Neill narrates the story of some of New Zealand's wildest places, home to hyper-intelligent parrots, snails with teeth, and constellations of glow-worms. In these often inhospitable locations, any creature hoping to survive must resort to radical techniques, none more so than on the South Island, which lies toward Antarctica and is home to the rapidly rising peaks of the Southern Alps(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world. With Katty Kay and Christian Fraser(n)

programme.title: Handmade
programme.desc: Filmed over five days, this programme examines the complex and time-consuming process involved in building a classic Windsor chair, made by craftsman Jim Steele in his Warwickshire workshop. The traditional design features woods chosen for their different qualities - ash, elm and yew - and the finished object has just two screws and no glue(n)

programme.title: King Alfred and the Anglo-Saxons
programme.desc: Michael Wood explores the role played by Alfred the Great's daughter Aethelflaed in England's battles in civil war and against the Vikings. The presenter contemplates how England 'may never have happened' without the young ruler of Mercia, who led armies, built fortresses, was a skilled diplomat in campaigning against the Norse invaders, and became a beloved leader in the British Isles(n)

programme.title: Royal History's Biggest Fibs with Lucy Worsley
programme.desc: The historian examines how writers, politicians and artists have manipulated the history of the Spanish Armada. She takes a look at the most famous parts of the story, from Francis Drake's game of bowls to Elizabeth I rousing her troops at Tilbury, and reveals how in Victorian times, the story was used as propaganda for the British Empire(n)

programme.title: Queen Elizabeth's Secret Agents
programme.desc: Documentary featuring dramatic reconstructions and interviews with historians to examine the network of spymasters and secret agents that helped protect Elizabeth I from assassination, terror and treason for more than 40 years. The first edition reveals how the pope's declaration of Elizabeth as a heretic 12 years into her reign set England's Catholics against her, leading the queen to charge William Cecil with the task of stopping assassins getting through, and the huge espionage network he established saw his spies break Catholic conspiracies at home and abroad(n)

programme.title: Armada - 12 Days to Save England
programme.desc: Three-part documentary about the sinking of the Spanish Armada, featuring dramatic reconstructions and information gleaned from recently recovered documents. Dan Snow boards a yacht on the English channel to get a sense of the naval battle, revealing how tensions between England and Spain were fuelled by religion, piracy and Elizabeth I's rejection of King Philip II of Spain as a suitor 30 years earlier, and how a missed opportunity could have resulted in disaster. Featuring Anita Dobson as the queen(n)

programme.title: Where in the World?
programme.desc: In this programme we visit three children who are all having a fascinating day out. In Russia, seven-year-old Liza picks blueberries with her grandma - and helps turn them into delicious pancakes. In Ghana, Tyra, who's five, goes to the market with her mum and learns how to do the shopping. And in Jordan, Hamza, who's eight, has a day out in town with his dad, and buys a lovely present for his baby sister, Mimi. We see that although children live in different countries, may look different and speak different languages, the joys and discoveries of childhood are universal.(n)

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Sister Julienne decides it is time to take matters in hand when a letter arrives from the council saying that it will cut funding to Nonnatus House and no longer pay for the accommodation, and goes to see Violet at one of her open sessions as a local councillor. At clinic, Nurse Crane meets and cares for heavily pregnant Bonnie, who is not married but committed to the baby's father Eddie, while Kevin's dependency on pain relief is discovered by the team and Dr Turner and Miss Higgins must find a way to help him(n)

programme.title: Last Tango in Halifax
programme.desc: Alan and Celia are not seeing eye to eye seven years into their marriage, while tempers fray at Caroline and Gillian's joint birthday when Celia can't hold her tongue. Return of Sally Wainwright's drama, starring Derek Jacobi, Anne Reid, Sarah Lancashire, Nicola Walker, Tony Gardner and Dean Andrews(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman presents highlights of the latest Premier League fixtures - Manchester United v Watford at Old Trafford, Wolverhampton Wanderers v Norwich City at Molineux and Arsenal v Everton at Emirates Stadium. The stakes were high for all six teams in action, with Watford and Norwich fighting for survival and the other sides all hoping to play European football next season. Plus, a round-up of yesterday's goals in the top flight(n)

programme.desc: Reshmin Chowdhury presents highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League, including Arsenal v Reading at Meadow Park and Manchester City v Chelsea at Academy Stadium, as the title race between the Gunners, City and the Blues continued(n)

programme.desc: Omar Hamdi finds out just how little lobster a restaurant chain puts in its pasta dish, while Lucy Owen is at Swansea market to learn more about the way the catering trade operates. Rachel Treadaway-Williams meets a widow left disappointed when an insurance policy didn't pay out, and there's help for Thomas Cook customers who were refused a refund(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Chantelle is shocked to learn Leo is dead and Whitney has been arrested. Elsewhere, Tiffany is concerned after speaking to Whitney and asks Gray to defend her case. Despite his reservations, Gray agrees to help. Jean grows frustrated as Daniel is blase about his situation(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: The inside story of the downfall of Alberto Salazar, who coached Britain's greatest track athlete to Olympic glory. Reporter Mark Daly first exposed doping at Salazar's Nike Oregon Project in 2015, and in this investigation he reveals fresh allegations about the disgraced coach and raises new questions about his relationship with Mo Farah(n)

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: John Torode and Gregg Wallace return with the cookery competition, as a new batch of contestants are put through gruelling culinary challenges to test not just their skills, but also their knowledge of food and ability to cope in a professional kitchen. This year they will have to cook not only for their place in the competition but also the right to wear a coveted MasterChef apron, with six contestants in each heat and only four aprons up for grabs, beginning by preparing their signature dish(n)

programme.title: EastEnders

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Ric is determined to do anything he can to help the relative of a former patient, despite the AAU being filled to capacity and the hospital being in the midst of a nursing crisis. Essie realises that she has to decide where she stands with Sasha when Ben makes a grand gesture. Kian returns from Holby from Canada to discover that Jac has been taking a step back from her work, and resolves to force her out of her comfort zone(n)

programme.title: Hidden
programme.desc: The community gathers to mourn the passing of Geraint Ellis, and Mia cannot keep away. That night, she and her friends visit an old ruin with a dark history. Crime drama, starring Sian Reese-Williams(n)

programme.title: The Split
programme.desc: Hannah finds her personal and professional lives colliding when she is forced to work with Nathan and his new protegee on the Hansen case. She desperately tries to clean up any evidence of her affair, only for Christie to uncover a secret about her past that leaves him consumed with anger - and secrets threaten to spill out at an anniversary party. The lawyers attend their first meeting with Richie's representatives to discuss childcare arrangements, which leaves Hannah concerned that her opponents will fight dirty and that Fi has not told her everything. Guest starring Anna Chancellor(n)

programme.desc: Four people contemplating cosmetic surgery get to witness a tummy tuck procedure being performed, and question if they still want to go through with it themselves. programme.desc: Matt Baker, Ellie Harrison and Steve Brown visit Shaftesbury in Dorset to take part in the town's annual snowdrop festival. Plus, Adam Henson meets a trailblazing vet(n)

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: In the aftermath of the Great CyberWar, the Doctor arrives in the far future, intent on protecting the last of the human race from the deadly Cybermen. But in the face of such a relentless enemy, has she put her best friends at risk? What terrors lie hiding in the depths of space, and what is Ko Sharmus? Guest starring Julie Graham, Patrick O'Kane and Steve Toussaint(n)

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Sister Julienne decides it is time to take matters in hand when a letter arrives from the council saying that it will cut funding to Nonnatus House and no longer pay for the accommodation, and goes to see Violet at one of her open sessions as a local councillor. At clinic, Nurse Crane meets and cares for heavily pregnant Bonnie, who is not married but committed to the baby's father Eddie, while Kevin's dependency on pain relief is discovered by the team and Dr Turner and Miss Higgins must find a way to help him(n)

programme.title: Last Tango in Halifax
programme.desc: Alan and Celia are not seeing eye to eye seven years into their marriage, while tempers fray at Caroline and Gillian's joint birthday when Celia can't hold her tongue. Return of Sally Wainwright's drama, starring Derek Jacobi, Anne Reid, Sarah Lancashire, Nicola Walker, Tony Gardner and Dean Andrews(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman presents highlights of the latest Premier League fixtures - Manchester United v Watford at Old Trafford, Wolverhampton Wanderers v Norwich City at Molineux and Arsenal v Everton at Emirates Stadium. The stakes were high for all six teams in action, with Watford and Norwich fighting for survival and the other sides all hoping to play European football next season. Plus, a round-up of yesterday's goals in the top flight(n)

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Reshmin Chowdhury presents highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League, including Arsenal v Reading at Meadow Park and Manchester City v Chelsea at Academy Stadium, as the title race between the Gunners, City and the Blues continued(n)

programme.title: Women's Six Nations Highlights
programme.desc: Sara Orchard presents action from the third round of fixtures, featuring Wales v France at Cardiff Arms Park, England v Ireland at Castle Park and Italy v Scotland at Stadio Giovanni Mari(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather. Presented by Dan Walker and Louise Minchin(n)

programme.title: Britain's Housing Scandal
programme.desc: Matt Allwright is in Liverpool to investigate the case of a vulnerable tenant trapped in a collapsing property, and meets a Birmingham resident who has had enough of the abandoned house next door(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts and the team explore properties about to be auctioned in Langley Mill in Derbyshire, Tylorstown in Rhondda Cynon Taf and Milton Regis in Kent(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under Revisited
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman catches up with an Australian-born man and his Colombian-born, wife whose trial week in Brisbane a year previously had proved traumatic for their daughter(n)

programme.title: Defenders UK
programme.desc: The enforcement officers investigate the tragic death of a Red Arrows pilot and smash a puppy-smuggling operation. Narrated by Harry Hepple(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Danny Sebastian and Caroline Hawley help two teams look for items at an antiques centre in King's Lynn, Norfolk, while presenter Eric Knowles meets an unlikely war hero(n)

programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Zara and Daniel confront Bear about his slippery business dealings, Karen brings Abz into the Mill and there is bad blood between three generations of a warring family(n)

programme.title: Shakespeare & Hathaway - Private Investigators
programme.desc: Frank and Lu must clear their names when they are framed for various crimes by some mysterious doppelgangers. Starring Mark Benton and Jo Joyner(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton helps newlyweds from London find a home in Surrey, and learns about a project at the British Wildlife Centre that is protecting an important native species(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: The Kent County Show's sheep competition sees farmers Ed and Gemma take to the ring, where their daughter Poppy has her sights set on first prize(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Experts Catherine Southon and Paul Laidlaw set off on a search for items in Nottinghamshire, heading for an auction showdown in Market Harborough, Leicestershire(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Matt Baker present the first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat(n)

programme.title: The Chronicles of Erne
programme.desc: Colin Morgan narrates a documentary following life on Upper and Lower Lough Erne over the course of the four seasons, beginning with spring. Retired school teacher Pat Lunny takes a trip out on his boat around his usual haunts around the islands near his home town of Enniskillen, while across the lough on White North Island, Amy Burns from RSPBNI is moving Highland cattle to some of the charity's other reserves(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Chantelle is shocked to learn Leo is dead and Whitney has been arrested. Elsewhere, Tiffany is concerned after speaking to Whitney and asks Gray to defend her case. Despite his reservations, Gray agrees to help. Jean grows frustrated as Daniel is blase about his situation(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: The inside story of the downfall of Alberto Salazar, who coached Britain's greatest track athlete to Olympic glory. Reporter Mark Daly first exposed doping at Salazar's Nike Oregon Project in 2015, and in this investigation he reveals fresh allegations about the disgraced coach and raises new questions about his relationship with Mo Farah(n)

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: John Torode and Gregg Wallace return with the cookery competition, as a new batch of contestants are put through gruelling culinary challenges to test not just their skills, but also their knowledge of food and ability to cope in a professional kitchen. This year they will have to cook not only for their place in the competition but also the right to wear a coveted MasterChef apron, with six contestants in each heat and only four aprons up for grabs, beginning by preparing their signature dish(n)

programme.title: True North
programme.desc: From big hairdos to online dress disasters, lavish limos to mahogany spray-on tans, the school prom has become an essential and expensive rite of passage for teenagers in Northern Ireland. This documentary following fifth and sixth formers across the country as they prepare for the biggest event in the school leavers' calendar(n)

programme.title: True North
programme.desc: Documentary following the teenage girls who are the driving force behind Northern Ireland's Strike for Climate movement(n)

programme.title: Community Life
programme.desc: This edition features Luminous Soul, a dance initiative for people with disabilities, and also includes information about volunteering opportunities with DeafblindNI(n)

programme.title: This Country

programme.title: Young, Welsh and Pretty Minted
programme.desc: The return of the documentary following young entrepreneurs in Wales, with Swansea fashion designer Alex Pike heading to Cannes Film and Fashion week. The programme also finds out what Toni and Gonth have been up to since the last series(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Gray feels the pressure after agreeing to take on Whitney's case and snaps at Mackenzie and Mia, leaving Chantelle worried. Following a conversation with Daniel, Jean hatches a plan to cheer him up. However, her mood changes following a phone call as she enlists help from Mo and Kush(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Ric is determined to do anything he can to help the relative of a former patient, despite the AAU being filled to capacity and the hospital being in the midst of a nursing crisis. Essie realises that she has to decide where she stands with Sasha when Ben makes a grand gesture. Kian returns from Holby from Canada to discover that Jac has been taking a step back from her work, and resolves to force her out of her comfort zone(n)

programme.title: The Split
programme.desc: Hannah finds her personal and professional lives colliding when she is forced to work with Nathan and his new protegee on the Hansen case. She desperately tries to clean up any evidence of her affair, only for Christie to uncover a secret about her past that leaves him consumed with anger - and secrets threaten to spill out at an anniversary party. The lawyers attend their first meeting with Richie's representatives to discuss childcare arrangements, which leaves Hannah concerned that her opponents will fight dirty and that Fi has not told her everything. Guest starring Anna Chancellor(n)

programme.title: The Voice UK
programme.desc: Spinning-chair singing contest, in which celebrity 'coaches' seek out the nation's best vocal talent. But there is a simple twist - with the four coaches selecting their teams through 'blind auditions', looks play no part in the selection process, meaning each singer is chosen on vocal ability alone(n)

programme.title: Ant & Dec's Saturday Night Takeaway
programme.desc: The popular duo return with a new series of the entertainment show, in which Ant v Dec goes global. This series will feature the biggest ever giveaway in British TV history and a finale coming from a very special location. In the first episode, Stephen Mulhern joins Ant and Dec in the studio(n)

programme.title: Six Nations Live
programme.desc: England v Ireland (Kick-off 3.00pm). Mark Pougatch presents all the action from the final match in the third round of fixtures, which takes place at Twickenham. England bounced back from a defeat at the hands of France by recording a 13-6 victory over Scotland at Murrayfield. However, they now face another tough test against the Irish, who are chasing the Triple Crown after wins against the Scots and Wales under new coach Andy Farrell. With analysis from Clive Woodward, Jonny Wilkinson and Brian O'Driscoll, pitchside reports by Martin Bayfield, and commentary from Nick Mullins, Lawrence Dallaglio and Gordon D'Arcy(n)

programme.title: Dancing on Ice
programme.desc: It's Movie Week as the remaining celebrities and their partners battle it out to stay in the competition and secure a place in the semi-final. Jayne Torvill and Christopher Dean head up the ice panel, alongside Ashley Banjo and John Barrowman, and there's a performance from Luke Evans alongside the professional skaters. Hosted by Phillip Schofield and Holly Willoughby(n)

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: When the detective is called to investigate what appears to be a freak accident at Lady Matilda's College, he uncovers a potential link between a series of peculiar incidents across Oxford. Despite Thursday's scepticism, he becomes convinced the accidents are the result of foul play. Lady Matilda's is in the middle of a referendum campaign to decide whether or not the college should become co-educational - and when a woman is attacked while walking alone on a towpath, Endeavour and Thursday try to find the assailant before they strike again. Drama, starring Shaun Evans(n)

programme.title: Cilla: The Lost Tapes
programme.desc: Following her death, Cilla Black's family found a stash of films and behind-the-scenes footage from her personal video collection, along with hours of audio recording of Cilla telling her life story. Narrated by Sheridan Smith, this documentary includes interviews with the family and friends who knew Cilla best, as they watch the lost footage for the first time and recall their memories of a TV icon(n)

programme.title: Harry Redknapp's Sandbanks Summer
programme.desc: Former football manager Harry Redknapp welcomes celebrity friends to his house in Sandbanks, Britain's most exclusive and expensive seaside resort, offering an insight into this unique part of the British south coast peninsula. In the first episode, Harry is joined by Neil ‘Razor' Ruddock and Joe Pasquale. Neil is considering a move to the area, so Harry takes him and Joe to see a very desirable £5 million penthouse with some of the best views Sandbanks has to offer(n)

programme.title: The BRIT Awards 2020
programme.desc: Jack Whitehall hosts the 40th edition of the annual music awards. Stormzy, Michael Kiwanuka, Lewis Capaldi, Dave and Harry Styles are in the running for Album of the Year, while Celeste has been announced as the recipient of this year's Rising Star Award. The show will feature performances from Mabel, Harry Styles, Stormzy, Billie Eilish, Lewis Capaldi and Celeste(n)

programme.title: Australian Wilderness with Ray Mears
programme.desc: Ray heads deep into the Australian Outback discover how both people and animals have developed ways to thrive in otherwise challenging conditions. He begins in Western Australia, where 20 million years ago, the sea bed erupted to create the great limestone cliffs of Ningaloo. Amid the rocks, the presenter encounters the fossils of giant prehistoric sharks, before swimming with their modern descendants - the whale sharks - out on Ningaloo reef(n)

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot(n)

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh asks the questions as contestants Paul, John, Hannah and Mary pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Rhona becomes frustrated when she is not taken seriously, Al and Kim receive some shocking news and Cain has disposed of the gun(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street

programme.title: The Martin Lewis Money Show
programme.desc: Last year one in every 20 people was a victim of fraud - with scammers stealing millions from the British public doing it increasingly via the internet. The financial journalist and Angellica Bell look at potential solutions for people who have been caught out and the need for greater online controls.(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Sarah informs Beth that Bethany will be starting work at the factory tomorrow and she expects her to be made welcome, Danny tells James he doesn't want to continue seeing him, and as Kevin awaits Abi's arrival for tea he is shocked to find Ray on his doorstep(n)

programme.title: Flesh and Blood
programme.desc: When recently widowed Vivien announces her love for a new man, Mark, it throws her family of adult children into chaos. The siblings' suspicions about Mark are fuelled by rising tensions in their own lives. Helen's focus on her job has led to a rift in her marriage, Jake's gambling debts have caused his wife and kids to leave him, and Natalie is lying to everybody about her secret relationship. Meanwhile, Vivien's neighbour, Mary, is simply trying to protect the family she has known for years. But in a few weeks one of these people will be lying on the beach, terribly injured - perhaps dead. Drama, starring Francesca Annis, Imelda Staunton and Stephen Rea(n)

programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents(n)

programme.desc: Vivien shocks her children by announcing she plans to sell their childhood home and go travelling with Mark. programme.title: Tyson Fury: The Gypsy King
programme.desc: The heavyweight celebrates a victory over Thomas Schwartz in Las Vegas by enjoying a night of heavy drinking back home in Morecambe and video-calling rival Anthony Joshua. This angers his wife Paris, who is reminded of his alcohol abuse during his years of depression, and Fury looks to make amends by treating the family to a day out in Blackpool and booking a family holiday. The boxer then heads to Marbella with trainer Ben Davison, before preparing to face his next opponent Otto Wallin back in Vegas, in what proves to be a challenging fight(n)

programme.title: Australian Wilderness with Ray Mears
programme.desc: Ray heads deep into the Australian Outback discover how both people and animals have developed ways to thrive in otherwise challenging conditions. He begins in Western Australia, where 20 million years ago, the sea bed erupted to create the great limestone cliffs of Ningaloo. Amid the rocks, the presenter encounters the fossils of giant prehistoric sharks, before swimming with their modern descendants - the whale sharks - out on Ningaloo reef(n)

programme.title: Flesh and Blood
programme.desc: When recently widowed Vivien announces her love for a new man, Mark, it throws her family of adult children into chaos. The siblings' suspicions about Mark are fuelled by rising tensions in their own lives. Helen's focus on her job has led to a rift in her marriage, Jake's gambling debts have caused his wife and kids to leave him, and Natalie is lying to everybody about her secret relationship. Meanwhile, Vivien's neighbour, Mary, is simply trying to protect the family she has known for years. But in a few weeks one of these people will be lying on the beach, terribly injured - perhaps dead. Drama, starring Francesca Annis, Imelda Staunton and Stephen Rea(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: An amateur archaeologist publishes a book about his father's discovery of Celtic artefacts in an ancient mound, and plans a celebration on the site - but landowner Gareth Heldman is less than keen on the idea. While out walking, Joyce spots Heldman arguing with a woman, and hours later he winds up stabbed to death with an old weapon that bears a striking resemblance to the one found during the dig. John Nettles and John Hopkins star(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: On doctor's orders, the dapper Belgian sleuth visits Brighton for what he hopes will be a relaxing holiday, but it leads to one of the most absorbing cases he has ever faced - the disappearance of a cache of jewels. Mystery, starring David Suchet, with Hermione Norris, Philip Jackson and Sorcha Cusack(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Coma victim Harriet Farmer finally wakes up a year after she was attacked while trying to elope, but her boyfriend Finn Robson is still missing. Soon after she regains consciousness, members of a chess club are targeted by a killer. Could the assault that left Harriet for dead be linked to moves on a games board? Guest starring Fay Ripley (Cold Feet), with Neil Dudgeon and Jason Hughes(n)

programme.title: The Cruise
programme.desc: Captain Perrin throws a huge party to celebrate the ship's first birthday, with executive chef Shaun creating a giant cake that is so heavy it takes 10 men to carry - and they soon come unstuck when they cannot fit the ship-shaped sponge into the lift. Passengers Norman and Maria are looking forward to attending the lavish formal party, although they will be going nowhere unless front-desk supervisor Herbert can locate their missing luggage, which contains his tuxedo and her party frock. Dancer Dulcie faces her staff appraisal, and a visit from her boyfriend helps with her homesickness(n)

programme.title: The Cruise
programme.desc: Romance is in the air for casino host Cecilia and assistant maitre d' Alberto - and she already has clear ideas about the size of her engagement ring. Things are not so happy for waitress Valeria, whose relationship ended when her boyfriend left the ship and now a close friend is leaving too. However, it isn't long until a new beau catches her eye. Timothy came on board after splitting from his partner of four years - but he has little time to worry about his broken heart in his new job with the shore excursions team, especially when he runs into hot water with a couple of misplaced passengers(n)

programme.title: Joanna Lumley's India
programme.desc: Joanna's adventures across India conclude as she explores Rajasthan, Delhi and the mountainous North West, before ending in Kashmir, the place of her birth. Her first stop takes her to the Ranthambhore National Park, where she learns about the Wildlife Protection Society of India's tiger conservation programmes. Joanna's next stop takes her to the city of Delhi, where she begins her exploration of the capital at Humayun's Tomb, before ending her 5000 mile journey in Srinagar in Kashmi, the place where she was born(n)

programme.title: The Good Karma Hospital
programme.desc: The clinic is pushed to its limits when a building collapses in Barco and the doctors face a desperate struggle to save the injured survivors. programme.title: The Good Karma Hospital

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Walker manages to arrest one member of a robber gang when a sting operation goes wrong - but ends up being taken hostage by the man's brother. The PC's disappearance is bad news for Blaketon, who's frantic to pull together a winning side for the annual cricket match against rivals Scarsdale. Cricketers Martin Bicknell, Ray Illingworth and Brian Close guest star, alongside Dickie Bird as the umpire(n)

programme.title: The Darling Buds of May
programme.desc: Part one of two. Pop befriends a pilot he rescues from his crashed plane, but suspects he is being drawn into a smuggling racket when the family is invited to a celebratory champagne night on the town. Drama, starring David Jason(n)

programme.title: That's My Boy
programme.desc: Ida is adamant she's not available for work when the employment agency gets in touch _ until she takes a call(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The detective discovers his half-sister's daughter has committed suicide and sets out to find what prompted it, entering the world of teenage rave parties and drug-taking. Meanwhile, Lewis has his sights set on promotion and studies for exams. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star, with Sorcha Cusack, Jason Isaacs and John Junkin(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: The police search for a boy who went missing after discovering his mother was having an affair. Younger strikes up an unlikely friendship as he fills in for the local lollipop lady, while Walker finds he isn't over Helen during a date with one of Gina's friends, and Carol's fiance Frank gets time off from the Navy to visit her. Sixties-set drama, with Jonathan Kerrigan and Lisa Kay(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The McAllisters row about Bernard's involvement with Kathy. Jack has some misgivings about Sarah's new job. Sarah and Jan plan a girls' night out(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Caroline realises that Eric has been trying to defraud Alan. Zak Dingle claims that Angharad assaulted Tina. Rachel and Chris agree to show a united front(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Maureen celebrates her six-month anniversary of running the shop. Steve is pleased when Fiona admits she likes him. Mavis is put out when Roger talks about Rita all the time(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Mavis is horrified to learn Roger wants to paint Rita's portrait. Maureen is sick of being stuck in the shop. Denise tells Ken she no longer wants him to be part of her life(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Scott investigate when a man collapses and dies outside a pub after a fight with his brother-in-law. The discovery of suspicious burn marks on the victim's forehead reveals sinister secrets and when a second body is found, the detectives are drawn into a world of superstition and family tensions. Murder mystery with John Nettles, John Hopkins and guest starring Geoffrey Whitehead(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: The police search for a boy who went missing after discovering his mother was having an affair. Younger strikes up an unlikely friendship as he fills in for the local lollipop lady, while Walker finds he isn't over Helen during a date with one of Gina's friends, and Carol's fiance Frank gets time off from the Navy to visit her. Sixties-set drama, with Jonathan Kerrigan and Lisa Kay(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: English country squire Marcus Waverly receives a series of anonymous letters threatening to kidnap his son Johnnie, but the case is dismissed by Scotland Yard - prompting Poirot to open his own investigation. Drama, starring David Suchet and Geoffrey Bateman(n)

programme.title: Maigret
programme.desc: The detective's latest case presents him with a baffling conundrum in the shape of two seemingly random murders, with the victims coming from very different ends of the social spectrum - one is a countess, and the other a showgirl. His investigation soon reveals a dark secret that is an unexpected connection between the two women, both of whom have past experiences involving the same hotel in Nice. Rowan Atkinson makes his fourth appearance as the French detective from Georges Simenon's series of novels(n)

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: Crime drama following the personal and professional lives of two detectives working for a Manchester police unit that specialises in murder cases. DC Janet Scott (Lesley Sharp) and DC Rachel Bailey (Suranne Jones) investigate the case of a pregnant woman who was killed in her bedroom. Meanwhile, Rachel's love life takes a turn for the worse and Janet gets a call that reawakens a past tragedy. Also starring Rupert Graves, Amelia Bullmore and Nicholas Gleaves(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The detective discovers his half-sister's daughter has committed suicide and sets out to find what prompted it, entering the world of teenage rave parties and drug-taking. Meanwhile, Lewis has his sights set on promotion and studies for exams. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star, with Sorcha Cusack, Jason Isaacs and John Junkin(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: A former bookie falls under suspicion for the murder of a hospital benefactor, who is found dead in his car. As the detectives investigate the case, it becomes clear their prime suspect had a strong motive, believing the victim was responsible for putting his daughter into a coma. Brian Cox guest stars, with John Thaw and Kevin Whately(n)
